overview: Nic Fit is a Legacy UBRG ramp deck that leverages mana acceleration and card selection to outpace opponents. By combining Veteran Explorer's mana generation with Green Sun's Zenith's targeted fetching, the deck establishes early board presence and scales into late-game threats. Cabal Therapy provides crucial card advantage and information to navigate complex matchups.
win_conditions: Generating massive mana advantage with Veteran Explorer to cast oversized threats
Using Green Sun's Zenith to fetch precise finishers or answers
Outvaluing opponents through repeated Cabal Therapy draws

keep_criteria: Hands with two or more lands and at least one ramp or interaction spell
Hands containing Veteran Explorer or Green Sun's Zenith with sufficient mana sources
Hands with early Cabal Therapy to secure information
Keep hands with 2-3 lands, Green Sun's Zenith, Cabal Therapy, and at least one early threat (Tarmogoyf, Scavenging Ooze, Veteran Explorer, or Bloodbraid Elf).
Prioritize hands that can cast a 1-mana spell on turn 1 and a 2-mana spell on turn 2.
mulligan_criteria: Hands with three or more lands and no ramp or interaction
Hands lacking any card that impacts the early game
Hands with only late-game threats and no acceleration
Mulligan hands with 0-1 lands, multiple copies of Therapy without threats, or hands lacking early interaction/pressure.

early_game: Prioritize land drops and cast ramp spells to enable turn-three or turn-four plays
Use Cabal Therapy to probe opponent's hand and identify key threats or answers
Hold up mana for potential interaction while establishing a minimal board presence
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Cabal Therapy targeting opponent's hand (discard a key disruption or combo piece) or cast a 1-mana threat like Tarmogoyf/Scavenging Ooze.
Turn 2: Play a second land, cast Green Sun's Zenith to tutor for a 2-3 mana threat (Veteran Explorer, Bloodbraid Elf, or Thalia's Guardian) or additional disruption.
Sequence lands to ensure double-colored mana availability for Zenith and threats
fetch lands should be used to find shock lands or duals that enter untapped if possible.
mid_game: Cast Veteran Explorer to generate excess mana and accelerate the curve
Deploy Green Sun's Zenith to find targeted threats, removal, or additional ramp based on Therapy information
Begin applying pressure with mid-sized creatures while maintaining card advantage
Treat Green Sun's Zenith as a premium resource
only cast it when you can guarantee a game-winning or game-swinging threat.
Use Cabal Therapy early to disrupt opponent's hand, saving it for key turns.
Manage your graveyard by avoiding unnecessary self-mill and using Surgical Extraction if needed.
Keep a hand of 3-4 cards to maintain flexibility against disruption.
Commit threats when the opponent has no sweepers or has exhausted their removal.
late_game: Flood the board with mana-accelerated threats that opponents cannot answer
Use repeated Cabal Therapy to strip opponent's remaining resources
Close out games by leveraging mana advantage to cast multiple threats per turn
Veteran Explorer (often pumped to 3/3 or 4/4), Tarmogoyf, and Bloodbraid Elf.
Secure victory by applying consistent pressure, using combat tricks to bypass removal, and leveraging Green Sun's Zenith to find a late-game finisher if needed.
Go all-in when the opponent has no sweepers, has used their removal on earlier threats, or is at low life.
Commit multiple threats if you can trade favorably or if you have combat tricks to protect your board.
Push for lethal with Veteran Explorer's pump ability when opponent has no blockers or removal.

key_cards.name: Veteran Explorer
key_cards.notes: Cast on curve to generate two additional mana per turn, enabling oversized spells and multiple Zenith casts

key_cards.name: Green Sun's Zenith
key_cards.notes: Use after Cabal Therapy to precisely target threats or answers needed for the current matchup

key_cards.name: Cabal Therapy
key_cards.notes: Cast early to reveal opponent's game plan, then use that information to optimize Zenith targets and sequencing

sequencing_tips: Cast Cabal Therapy before Green Sun's Zenith to know exactly which creature or spell to fetch
Play Veteran Explorer as early as possible to maximize cumulative mana generation
Hold up black mana for potential interaction while keeping enough open to cast ramp on curve
Prioritize fetching threats that pressure the opponent's weakest color or matchup weakness
Green Sun's Zenith is a one-sided tutor, so know your topdecks and what you're looking for.
Cabal Therapy's downside is manageable early but can be a liability late
Veteran Explorer's pump ability is crucial in combat and should be saved for favorable trades or lethal.
Avoid overcommitting to the board without assessing sweepers.
Practice hand management to avoid being out of resources against control.

matchups.advice: Ramp quickly to large threats and use Cabal Therapy to strip early removal
Use Green Sun's Zenith to find answers to counterspells and race with mana advantage
Disrupt key combo pieces with Cabal Therapy and race with accelerated threats

Use multiple threats to force through, cast Green Sun's Zenith on your turn to bypass counters, and use Cabal Therapy to discard counterspells. If possible, tutor for Rest in Peace or Thalia's Guardian to lock them out. Use combat tricks to trade favorably if a threat is countered.

Apply early pressure to race their mana acceleration. Use Cabal Therapy to discard mana dorks or key ramp spells. Bring in Rest in Peace and Thalia's Guardian to neutralize their advantage. Use Green Sun's Zenith to find hate bears or additional disruption.

Value exchange is key; use Cabal Therapy to disrupt their Green Sun's Zenith or key threats. Track their hand for combat tricks and discard them. Use Veteran Explorer's pump ability to trade favorably. Don't overextend; play one threat at a time to bait removal. Use Green Sun's Zenith to find a superior threat or hate bear when they commit.

common_threats: Watch for counterspells (Force of Will, Daze, Spell Pierce) and targeted removal (Path to Exile, Swords to Plowshares, Lightning Axe).
Track opponent's hand size and discarded cards to anticipate their game plan.
Note if they're playing lands that suggest ramp (Tron) or combo (Sneak and Show).
Pay attention to when they tap out for spells, indicating vulnerability to pressure.
